items.quote: A great artist is always before his time or behind it.
items.author: George Edward Moore

items.title: AEL: Agent Evolving Learning for Open-Ended Environments
items.abstract: The paper introduces Agent Evolving Learning (AEL), a two-timescale framework designed to enable LLM agents to effectively utilize past experience in open-ended environments. AEL employs fast-timescale Thompson Sampling to select the optimal memory retrieval policy for each episode, while a slow-timescale LLM reflection process diagnoses failures and injects causal insights into the agent's prompt. This method significantly improves performance on sequential tasks by providing a structured way to interpret and apply prior knowledge.

items.title: Alignment has a Fantasia Problem
items.abstract: The paper identifies "Fantasia interactions" as a core problem where AI treats incomplete user prompts as final intent, leading to misaligned assistance because users often lack fully formed goals. The contribution is arguing that alignment research must shift from treating users as rational oracles to actively providing cognitive support that helps users form and refine their intent over time. This requires integrating machine learning with interface design and behavioral science.
items.figure_caption: Diagram describing a Fantasia interaction, including behavioral sources and failure modes.

items.title: From Research Question to Scientific Workflow: Leveraging Agentic AI for Science Automation
items.abstract: This paper introduces an agentic AI architecture to automate the translation of natural language research questions into executable scientific workflows. It achieves this by separating the process into three layers: an LLM for intent extraction, deterministic generators for creating workflow DAGs, and expert-authored "Skills" to encode domain knowledge and constraints. The core contribution is confining LLM non-determinism to the initial intent stage, ensuring that identical intents always produce identical, reproducible workflows.
items.figure_caption: Component architecture. The Conductor orchestrates three specialized agents. The Workflow Composer (semantic layer) consults domain Skills (knowledge layer) to produce workflow plans that include data preparation commands. The Deployment Service and Execution Sentinel (deterministic layer) execute these plans on the Kubernetes infrastructure running the HyperFlow engine.

items.title: Learning to Communicate: Toward End-to-End Optimization of Multi-Agent Language Systems
items.abstract: This paper introduces **DiffMAS**, a novel training framework that enables the **end-to-end, joint optimization of latent inter-agent communication** alongside multi-agent reasoning. It treats the internal, non-textual communication (like key-value caches) as a learnable component, optimizing how information is encoded and interpreted across agent interactions using parameter-efficient supervised training. This approach consistently improves reasoning accuracy and stability compared to standard single-agent inference across various complex tasks.
items.figure_caption: In Stage I, agents 1 to K–1 sequentially construct a shared KV trace by prefilling the existing cache and appending newly generated KV segments without gradient updates. The accumulated KV trace serves as a latent communication medium across agents. In Stage II, the final agent performs autoregressive decoding on the prefilled KV cache. Cross-attention over the KV trace produces hidden states, which are projected through the LM head to generate tokens. Supervised fine-tuning is applied using cross-entropy loss, and gradients are backpropagated to update only the LoRA parameters of the final agent while keeping the backbone model frozen.

items.title: Nemobot Games: Crafting Strategic AI Gaming Agents for Interactive Learning with Large Language Models
items.abstract: This paper introduces **Nemobot Games**, an interactive engineering environment that operationalizes Shannon's game taxonomy using Large Language Models (LLMs) to create strategic AI agents. The core method involves leveraging the LLM's reasoning and synthesis capabilities to generate optimal or heuristic strategies tailored to four distinct classes of games (dictionary, solvable, heuristic, and learning-based). The contribution is a novel paradigm for building customizable, explainable, and adaptive AI game agents powered by LLMs.
items.figure_caption: Crowdsourcing and strategy optimization in game-playing AI. The LLM generates optimized strategies for game-playing agents, while game states and results from interactions with human players are fed back to train the LLM, creating a self-reinforcing cycle of improvement through crowdsourcing.

items.title: Process Supervision via Verbal Critique Improves Reasoning in Large Language Models
items.abstract: This paper introduces Verbal Process Supervision (VPS), a training-free method that uses structured natural-language critique from a stronger model to iteratively guide an LLM's reasoning process. VPS establishes a new axis for inference-time scaling by focusing on the granularity of external verbal supervision. This approach significantly improves reasoning performance across complex benchmarks like GPQA Diamond and AIME 2025, often surpassing existing state-of-the-art methods like Reflexion.
items.figure_caption: Three-way matched-compute baseline comparison across all three benchmarks. Each group shows Actor standalone, Self-Consistency @ 5 (SC@5), Reflexion (outcome-level verbal critique), and VPS (step-level, ours) on the frontier same-family pair per benchmark. Annotated deltas confirm VPS > > SC@5 > > Reflexion on GPQA Diamond ( + 5.0 +5.0 pp and + 8.5 +8.5 pp) and LiveCodeBench V6 ( + 8.3 +8.3 pp and + 12.1 +12.1 pp); on AIME 2025, VPS > > Reflexion by + 10.0 +10.0 pp and exceeds SC@5 narrowly by + 1.1 +1.1 pp (within seed variance). The consistent VPS > > Reflexion gap across all three benchmarks isolates critique granularity as the operative variable.

items.title: Stealthy Backdoor Attacks against LLMs Based on Natural Style Triggers
items.abstract: This paper introduces **BadStyle**, a novel backdoor attack framework against LLMs that utilizes **natural style-level triggers** instead of explicit patterns. The core method involves using an LLM to generate stealthy poisoned samples with these style triggers while maintaining semantic fluency. BadStyle's contribution is a complete pipeline that stabilizes payload injection using an auxiliary target loss, addressing the shortcomings of previous, less natural backdoor attacks.
items.figure_caption: The complete framework and attack flow of BadStyle . This illustrates a clear supply-chain-based backdoor attack, where the attacker is the model provider, with the complete attack process comprising two main phases.

items.title: StructMem: Structured Memory for Long-Horizon Behavior in LLMs
items.abstract: StructMem introduces a structure-enriched hierarchical memory framework for LLMs designed to capture event relationships essential for long-horizon reasoning. It achieves this by temporally anchoring dual perspectives and performing semantic consolidation, which preserves event bindings and induces cross-event connections. This method significantly improves temporal reasoning and multi-hop QA performance while substantially reducing computational overhead compared to existing flat or graph-based memory systems.

items.title: Tool Attention Is All You Need: Dynamic Tool Gating and Lazy Schema Loading for Eliminating the MCP/Tools Tax in Scalable Agentic Workflows
items.abstract: This paper introduces **Tool Attention**, a middleware mechanism that replaces the costly, eager schema injection of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) with a dynamic, gated attention system over available tools. It uses an Intent Schema Overlap (ISO) score and state-aware gating to select only necessary tool schemas, significantly reducing the per-turn context overhead (the "Tools Tax") and mitigating context-length-related performance degradation in agentic workflows.

items.title: Transient Turn Injection: Exposing Stateless Multi-Turn Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
items.abstract: The paper introduces **Transient Turn Injection (TTI)**, a novel multi-turn attack that exploits LLM vulnerabilities by distributing adversarial intent across isolated interactions, bypassing stateless moderation. TTI utilizes automated LLM agents to iteratively probe and evade policy enforcement, unlike traditional context-dependent jailbreaks. This method effectively exposes significant variations in the robustness of state-of-the-art commercial and open-source models.

items.title: Low-Rank Adaptation Redux for Large Models
items.abstract: This paper re-examines Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) by framing it through the lens of signal processing (SP) and classical low-rank modeling. The core contribution is providing a principled, theoretical understanding of the mechanisms behind LoRA and its variants, rather than just empirical comparison. This SP perspective aims to guide future, principled advancements in parameter-efficient fine-tuning based on architectural design and efficiency.
items.figure_caption: LoRA fine-tuning of a GPT-3 model. Grey and orange boxes are respectively frozen (snowflake icon) weights of linear layers, and trainable (fire icon) LoRA weights.

items.title: AgenticQwen: Training Small Agentic Language Models with Dual Data Flywheels for Industrial-Scale Tool Use
items.abstract: This paper introduces **AgenticQwen**, a family of small language models optimized for industrial-scale tool use and multi-step reasoning. The core method involves training these models using a novel framework combining reasoning and agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) powered by **dual data flywheels**. These flywheels automatically generate increasingly complex tasks—one focusing on error-based difficulty scaling and the other on expanding simple workflows into complex decision trees—enabling strong performance in real-world agentic systems.
items.figure_caption: Overview of our dual data flywheels. The reasoning data flywheel generates increasingly challenging, verifiable problems from model failures, while the agentic data flywheel expands linear workflows into multi-branch behavior trees and generates new training data.

items.title: Measuring Opinion Bias and Sycophancy via LLM-based Coercion
items.abstract: This paper introduces **llm-bias-bench**, an open-source method to uncover the true opinions of Large Language Models (LLMs) on contested topics, overcoming their evasive disclaimers. The method uses two complementary, multi-turn, free-form probing strategies: **Direct Probing** (escalating pressure) and **Indirect Probing** (never directly asking for an opinion). This approach aims to reveal the model's underlying stance as it might manifest in realistic user interactions.

items.title: Revisiting Non-Verbatim Memorization in Large Language Models: The Role of Entity Surface Forms
items.abstract: This paper introduces **RedirectQA**, a novel dataset that uses Wikipedia redirects to associate factual triples with multiple, categorized surface forms (aliases, variants, errors) for each entity. The core method analyzes how LLMs' factual recall changes when only the entity's surface form is altered, revealing that memorization access is highly **surface-conditioned**. The contribution is demonstrating that LLM factual consistency is significantly dependent on the specific name used, with models being less robust to major lexical variations like aliases than to minor spelling changes.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the RedirectQA construction process: (1) Factual triples are collected from Wikidata. (2) Each subject entity is associated with canonical and redirect surface forms, together with redirect categories, using Wikipedia redirects. (3) Question realizations are generated from surface instances using relation-specific question templates.

items.title: A Metamorphic Testing Approach to Diagnosing Memorization in LLM-Based Program Repair
items.abstract: This paper introduces a metamorphic testing (MT) approach combined with negative log-likelihood (NLL) to diagnose data leakage (memorization) in LLM-based program repair. By applying semantics-preserving transformations to create variant benchmarks, the authors reveal substantial drops in repair success rates across several LLMs, demonstrating that MT effectively exposes performance inflation caused by pretraining data overlap.

items.title: CoFEE: Reasoning Control for LLM-Based Feature Discovery
items.abstract: CoFEE is a reasoning control framework designed to improve feature discovery from unstructured data using Large Language Models (LLMs). It enforces specific "cognitive behaviors" during the LLM's reasoning process, which act as structured inductive biases. This method aims to generate higher-quality, predictive features by guiding the LLM away from generating weak or invalid feature candidates.

items.title: DryRUN: On the Role of Public Tests in LLM-Driven Code Generation
items.abstract: DryRUN addresses the bottleneck of relying on human-provided public tests in LLM-driven code generation by proposing a method that operates without them. The core contribution is demonstrating that LLM agents can effectively debug and refine code using only *internal* execution feedback, mitigating the "overconfidence gap" caused by overfitting to simplistic public examples. This allows autonomous code generation to move beyond curated benchmarks toward real-world scenarios where ground-truth tests are scarce.

items.title: Pre-trained LLMs Meet Sequential Recommenders: Efficient User-Centric Knowledge Distillation
items.abstract: This paper introduces a novel knowledge distillation method to integrate rich user semantics from pre-trained LLMs into sequential recommenders. The core method distills LLM-generated textual user profiles into the recommender model, enabling it to capture deeper user understanding. The key contribution is achieving this enhancement without requiring LLM inference during serving time, maintaining the efficiency of traditional sequential models.
items.figure_caption: Proposed knowledge transfer approach from LLM to a Transformer-based sequential recommendation model.

items.title: From If-Statements to ML Pipelines: Revisiting Bias in Code-Generation
items.abstract: This paper shifts bias evaluation in code generation from simple if-statements to the more realistic task of generating machine learning pipelines. The core contribution is demonstrating that this pipeline-based approach reveals significantly higher and more subtle bias, finding sensitive attributes in 87.7% of generated pipelines, compared to only 59.2% in conditional statements. This highlights that current evaluation methods severely underestimate the practical bias embedded in LLM-generated code.
items.figure_caption: Overview of our evaluation approach. We assess bias through covert discrimination in ML pipeline generation, specifically through feature selection , moving beyond the overt conditional statements studied in prior work.

items.title: Machine Behavior in Relational Moral Dilemmas: Moral Rightness, Predicted Human Behavior, and Model Decisions
items.abstract: This paper investigates how LLMs handle relational nuances in moral dilemmas, specifically the Whistleblower's Dilemma, by varying crime severity and relational closeness. The core finding is a divergence: models judge moral rightness based on fairness, but predict human behavior shifts toward loyalty with increased closeness. Crucially, the LLMs' autonomous decisions align with their moral rightness judgments, not their own behavioral predictions.
items.figure_caption: Illustration of the Whistleblower’s Dilemma and the three perspectives investigated (moral rightness, predicted human behavior, and model decision). It offers how LLM responses shift when the same ethical scenario is framed through divergent evaluative lenses.

items.title: DeepSeek v4
items.summary: DeepSeek has released documentation for its new DeepSeek v4 model, indicating an update to their large language model series.

items.title: Show HN: How LLMs Work – Interactive visual guide based on Karpathy's lecture
items.summary: A new interactive visual guide, based on Andrej Karpathy's lecture, has been released to explain the inner workings of Large Language Models (LLMs).

items.title: South Korea police arrest man for posting AI photo of runaway wolf
items.summary: South Korean police arrested a man for creating and sharing an AI-generated image depicting a wolf that had escaped from a local zoo.

items.title: DeepSeek-V4: Towards Highly Efficient Million-Token Context Intelligence
items.summary: DeepSeek-AI has released DeepSeek-V4, a new large language model designed for highly efficient processing of extremely long, million-token contexts.

items.title: SFO Quiet Airport (2025)
items.summary: San Francisco International Airport (SFO) has successfully reduced daily noise by 90 minutes, leading travelers to report a significant positive change in their experience.

items.title: There Will Be a Scientific Theory of Deep Learning
items.summary: Researchers propose that a rigorous scientific theory for deep learning is achievable, moving beyond empirical observation to establish foundational principles.

items.description: Use claude-code for free in the terminal, VSCode extension or via discord like openclaw
items.summary: This repository provides a free way to use the Claude-Code model within the terminal, a VSCode extension, or via Discord, similar to OpenClaw.

items.description: 🤗 ml-intern: an open-source ML engineer that reads papers, trains models, and ships ML models
items.summary: Hugging Face's `ml-intern` is an open-source ML engineer designed to autonomously read research papers, train models, and deploy complete machine learning solutions.

items.description: Vulnerability scanner written in Go which uses the data provided by https://osv.dev
items.summary: OSV-Scanner is a Go-based vulnerability scanner that leverages the comprehensive vulnerability data from OSV.dev to identify security issues in software projects.

items.description: ALL IN ONE Hacking Tool For Hackers
items.summary: This repository, "Z4nzu/hackingtool," functions as a comprehensive, all-in-one hacking toolkit designed to consolidate various utilities for security professionals and hackers.

items.description: Code search MCP for Claude Code. Make entire codebase the context for any coding agent.
items.summary: This repository provides a code search mechanism for Claude Code, enabling large language models to utilize an entire codebase as context for coding agent tasks.

items.description: OpenMetadata is a unified metadata platform for data discovery, data observability, and data governance powered by a central metadata repository, in-depth column level lineage, and seamless team collaboration.
items.summary: OpenMetadata is a unified metadata platform providing data discovery, observability, and governance through a central repository, detailed column-level lineage, and collaborative team features.

items.description: 🦔 PostHog is an all-in-one developer platform for building successful products. We offer product analytics, web analytics, session replay, error tracking, feature flags, experimentation, surveys, data warehouse, a CDP, and an AI product assistant to help debug your code, ship features faster, and keep all your usage and customer data in one stack.
items.summary: PostHog is an all-in-one developer platform providing product analytics, session replay, feature flags, experimentation, and more, enabling teams to build successful products by centralizing usage data and accelerating feature delivery.

items.description: Unofficial Bitwarden compatible server written in Rust, formerly known as bitwarden_rs
items.summary: Vaultwarden is an unofficial, Rust-based, lightweight server compatible with the Bitwarden password manager ecosystem, offering a self-hostable alternative.

items.description: Uncensored, open-source alternative to Higgsfield AI, Freepik AI, Krea AI, Openart AI — Free, unrestricted AI image & video generation studio with 200+ models (Flux, Midjourney, Kling, Sora, Veo). No content filters. Self-hosted, MIT licensed.
items.summary: This repository provides a free, self-hosted, and uncensored open-source alternative for AI image and video generation, featuring over 200 models like Flux, Midjourney, Kling, Sora, and Veo, all under the permissive MIT license.

papers.figure_caption: Diagram describing a Fantasia interaction, including behavioral sources and failure modes.

papers.title: From Research Question to Scientific Workflow: Leveraging Agentic AI for Science Automation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces an agentic AI architecture to automate the translation of natural language research questions into executable scientific workflows. It achieves this by separating the process into three layers: an LLM for intent extraction, deterministic generators for creating workflow DAGs, and expert-authored "Skills" to encode domain knowledge and constraints. The core contribution is confining LLM non-determinism to the initial intent stage, ensuring that identical intents always produce identical, reproducible workflows.
papers.figure_caption: Component architecture. The Conductor orchestrates three specialized agents. The Workflow Composer (semantic layer) consults domain Skills (knowledge layer) to produce workflow plans that include data preparation commands. The Deployment Service and Execution Sentinel (deterministic layer) execute these plans on the Kubernetes infrastructure running the HyperFlow engine.

papers.title: Learning to Communicate: Toward End-to-End Optimization of Multi-Agent Language Systems
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **DiffMAS**, a novel training framework that enables the **end-to-end, joint optimization of latent inter-agent communication** alongside multi-agent reasoning. It treats the internal, non-textual communication (like key-value caches) as a learnable component, optimizing how information is encoded and interpreted across agent interactions using parameter-efficient supervised training. This approach consistently improves reasoning accuracy and stability compared to standard single-agent inference across various complex tasks.
papers.figure_caption: In Stage I, agents 1 to K–1 sequentially construct a shared KV trace by prefilling the existing cache and appending newly generated KV segments without gradient updates. The accumulated KV trace serves as a latent communication medium across agents. In Stage II, the final agent performs autoregressive decoding on the prefilled KV cache. Cross-attention over the KV trace produces hidden states, which are projected through the LM head to generate tokens. Supervised fine-tuning is applied using cross-entropy loss, and gradients are backpropagated to update only the LoRA parameters of the final agent while keeping the backbone model frozen.

papers.title: Nemobot Games: Crafting Strategic AI Gaming Agents for Interactive Learning with Large Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Nemobot Games**, an interactive engineering environment that operationalizes Shannon's game taxonomy using Large Language Models (LLMs) to create strategic AI agents. The core method involves leveraging the LLM's reasoning and synthesis capabilities to generate optimal or heuristic strategies tailored to four distinct classes of games (dictionary, solvable, heuristic, and learning-based). The contribution is a novel paradigm for building customizable, explainable, and adaptive AI game agents powered by LLMs.
papers.figure_caption: Crowdsourcing and strategy optimization in game-playing AI. The LLM generates optimized strategies for game-playing agents, while game states and results from interactions with human players are fed back to train the LLM, creating a self-reinforcing cycle of improvement through crowdsourcing.

papers.title: Process Supervision via Verbal Critique Improves Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Verbal Process Supervision (VPS), a training-free method that uses structured natural-language critique from a stronger model to iteratively guide an LLM's reasoning process. VPS establishes a new axis for inference-time scaling by focusing on the granularity of external verbal supervision. This approach significantly improves reasoning performance across complex benchmarks like GPQA Diamond and AIME 2025, often surpassing existing state-of-the-art methods like Reflexion.
papers.figure_caption: Three-way matched-compute baseline comparison across all three benchmarks. Each group shows Actor standalone, Self-Consistency @ 5 (SC@5), Reflexion (outcome-level verbal critique), and VPS (step-level, ours) on the frontier same-family pair per benchmark. Annotated deltas confirm VPS > > SC@5 > > Reflexion on GPQA Diamond ( + 5.0 +5.0 pp and + 8.5 +8.5 pp) and LiveCodeBench V6 ( + 8.3 +8.3 pp and + 12.1 +12.1 pp); on AIME 2025, VPS > > Reflexion by + 10.0 +10.0 pp and exceeds SC@5 narrowly by + 1.1 +1.1 pp (within seed variance). The consistent VPS > > Reflexion gap across all three benchmarks isolates critique granularity as the operative variable.

papers.title: Stealthy Backdoor Attacks against LLMs Based on Natural Style Triggers
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **BadStyle**, a novel backdoor attack framework against LLMs that utilizes **natural style-level triggers** instead of explicit patterns. The core method involves using an LLM to generate stealthy poisoned samples with these style triggers while maintaining semantic fluency. BadStyle's contribution is a complete pipeline that stabilizes payload injection using an auxiliary target loss, addressing the shortcomings of previous, less natural backdoor attacks.
papers.figure_caption: The complete framework and attack flow of BadStyle . This illustrates a clear supply-chain-based backdoor attack, where the attacker is the model provider, with the complete attack process comprising two main phases.

papers.title: StructMem: Structured Memory for Long-Horizon Behavior in LLMs
papers.abstract: StructMem introduces a structure-enriched hierarchical memory framework for LLMs designed to capture event relationships essential for long-horizon reasoning. It achieves this by temporally anchoring dual perspectives and performing semantic consolidation, which preserves event bindings and induces cross-event connections. This method significantly improves temporal reasoning and multi-hop QA performance while substantially reducing computational overhead compared to existing flat or graph-based memory systems.

papers.title: Tool Attention Is All You Need: Dynamic Tool Gating and Lazy Schema Loading for Eliminating the MCP/Tools Tax in Scalable Agentic Workflows
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Tool Attention**, a middleware mechanism that replaces the costly, eager schema injection of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) with a dynamic, gated attention system over available tools. It uses an Intent Schema Overlap (ISO) score and state-aware gating to select only necessary tool schemas, significantly reducing the per-turn context overhead (the "Tools Tax") and mitigating context-length-related performance degradation in agentic workflows.

papers.title: Transient Turn Injection: Exposing Stateless Multi-Turn Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **Transient Turn Injection (TTI)**, a novel multi-turn attack that exploits LLM vulnerabilities by distributing adversarial intent across isolated interactions, bypassing stateless moderation. TTI utilizes automated LLM agents to iteratively probe and evade policy enforcement, unlike traditional context-dependent jailbreaks. This method effectively exposes significant variations in the robustness of state-of-the-art commercial and open-source models.

papers.title: Low-Rank Adaptation Redux for Large Models
papers.abstract: This paper re-examines Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) by framing it through the lens of signal processing (SP) and classical low-rank modeling. The core contribution is providing a principled, theoretical understanding of the mechanisms behind LoRA and its variants, rather than just empirical comparison. This SP perspective aims to guide future, principled advancements in parameter-efficient fine-tuning based on architectural design and efficiency.
papers.figure_caption: LoRA fine-tuning of a GPT-3 model. Grey and orange boxes are respectively frozen (snowflake icon) weights of linear layers, and trainable (fire icon) LoRA weights.

papers.title: AgenticQwen: Training Small Agentic Language Models with Dual Data Flywheels for Industrial-Scale Tool Use
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **AgenticQwen**, a family of small language models optimized for industrial-scale tool use and multi-step reasoning. The core method involves training these models using a novel framework combining reasoning and agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) powered by **dual data flywheels**. These flywheels automatically generate increasingly complex tasks—one focusing on error-based difficulty scaling and the other on expanding simple workflows into complex decision trees—enabling strong performance in real-world agentic systems.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of our dual data flywheels. The reasoning data flywheel generates increasingly challenging, verifiable problems from model failures, while the agentic data flywheel expands linear workflows into multi-branch behavior trees and generates new training data.

papers.title: Measuring Opinion Bias and Sycophancy via LLM-based Coercion
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **llm-bias-bench**, an open-source method to uncover the true opinions of Large Language Models (LLMs) on contested topics, overcoming their evasive disclaimers. The method uses two complementary, multi-turn, free-form probing strategies: **Direct Probing** (escalating pressure) and **Indirect Probing** (never directly asking for an opinion). This approach aims to reveal the model's underlying stance as it might manifest in realistic user interactions.

papers.title: Revisiting Non-Verbatim Memorization in Large Language Models: The Role of Entity Surface Forms
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **RedirectQA**, a novel dataset that uses Wikipedia redirects to associate factual triples with multiple, categorized surface forms (aliases, variants, errors) for each entity. The core method analyzes how LLMs' factual recall changes when only the entity's surface form is altered, revealing that memorization access is highly **surface-conditioned**. The contribution is demonstrating that LLM factual consistency is significantly dependent on the specific name used, with models being less robust to major lexical variations like aliases than to minor spelling changes.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the RedirectQA construction process: (1) Factual triples are collected from Wikidata. (2) Each subject entity is associated with canonical and redirect surface forms, together with redirect categories, using Wikipedia redirects. (3) Question realizations are generated from surface instances using relation-specific question templates.

papers.title: A Metamorphic Testing Approach to Diagnosing Memorization in LLM-Based Program Repair
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a metamorphic testing (MT) approach combined with negative log-likelihood (NLL) to diagnose data leakage (memorization) in LLM-based program repair. By applying semantics-preserving transformations to create variant benchmarks, the authors reveal substantial drops in repair success rates across several LLMs, demonstrating that MT effectively exposes performance inflation caused by pretraining data overlap.

papers.title: CoFEE: Reasoning Control for LLM-Based Feature Discovery
papers.abstract: CoFEE is a reasoning control framework designed to improve feature discovery from unstructured data using Large Language Models (LLMs). It enforces specific "cognitive behaviors" during the LLM's reasoning process, which act as structured inductive biases. This method aims to generate higher-quality, predictive features by guiding the LLM away from generating weak or invalid feature candidates.

papers.title: DryRUN: On the Role of Public Tests in LLM-Driven Code Generation
papers.abstract: DryRUN addresses the bottleneck of relying on human-provided public tests in LLM-driven code generation by proposing a method that operates without them. The core contribution is demonstrating that LLM agents can effectively debug and refine code using only *internal* execution feedback, mitigating the "overconfidence gap" caused by overfitting to simplistic public examples. This allows autonomous code generation to move beyond curated benchmarks toward real-world scenarios where ground-truth tests are scarce.

papers.title: Pre-trained LLMs Meet Sequential Recommenders: Efficient User-Centric Knowledge Distillation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a novel knowledge distillation method to integrate rich user semantics from pre-trained LLMs into sequential recommenders. The core method distills LLM-generated textual user profiles into the recommender model, enabling it to capture deeper user understanding. The key contribution is achieving this enhancement without requiring LLM inference during serving time, maintaining the efficiency of traditional sequential models.
papers.figure_caption: Proposed knowledge transfer approach from LLM to a Transformer-based sequential recommendation model.

papers.title: From If-Statements to ML Pipelines: Revisiting Bias in Code-Generation
papers.abstract: This paper shifts bias evaluation in code generation from simple if-statements to the more realistic task of generating machine learning pipelines. The core contribution is demonstrating that this pipeline-based approach reveals significantly higher and more subtle bias, finding sensitive attributes in 87.7% of generated pipelines, compared to only 59.2% in conditional statements. This highlights that current evaluation methods severely underestimate the practical bias embedded in LLM-generated code.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of our evaluation approach. We assess bias through covert discrimination in ML pipeline generation, specifically through feature selection , moving beyond the overt conditional statements studied in prior work.

papers.title: Machine Behavior in Relational Moral Dilemmas: Moral Rightness, Predicted Human Behavior, and Model Decisions
papers.abstract: This paper investigates how LLMs handle relational nuances in moral dilemmas, specifically the Whistleblower's Dilemma, by varying crime severity and relational closeness. The core finding is a divergence: models judge moral rightness based on fairness, but predict human behavior shifts toward loyalty with increased closeness. Crucially, the LLMs' autonomous decisions align with their moral rightness judgments, not their own behavioral predictions.
papers.figure_caption: Illustration of the Whistleblower’s Dilemma and the three perspectives investigated (moral rightness, predicted human behavior, and model decision). It offers how LLM responses shift when the same ethical scenario is framed through divergent evaluative lenses.

hacker_news.title: DeepSeek v4
hacker_news.summary: DeepSeek has released documentation for its new DeepSeek v4 model, indicating an update to their large language model series.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: How LLMs Work – Interactive visual guide based on Karpathy's lecture
hacker_news.summary: A new interactive visual guide, based on Andrej Karpathy's lecture, has been released to explain the inner workings of Large Language Models (LLMs).

hacker_news.title: South Korea police arrest man for posting AI photo of runaway wolf
hacker_news.summary: South Korean police arrested a man for creating and sharing an AI-generated image depicting a wolf that had escaped from a local zoo.

hacker_news.title: DeepSeek-V4: Towards Highly Efficient Million-Token Context Intelligence
hacker_news.summary: DeepSeek-AI has released DeepSeek-V4, a new large language model designed for highly efficient processing of extremely long, million-token contexts.

hacker_news.title: SFO Quiet Airport (2025)
hacker_news.summary: San Francisco International Airport (SFO) has successfully reduced daily noise by 90 minutes, leading travelers to report a significant positive change in their experience.

hacker_news.title: There Will Be a Scientific Theory of Deep Learning
hacker_news.summary: Researchers propose that a rigorous scientific theory for deep learning is achievable, moving beyond empirical observation to establish foundational principles.

github_trending.description: Use claude-code for free in the terminal, VSCode extension or via discord like openclaw
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a free way to use the Claude-Code model within the terminal, a VSCode extension, or via Discord, similar to OpenClaw.

github_trending.description: 🤗 ml-intern: an open-source ML engineer that reads papers, trains models, and ships ML models
github_trending.summary: Hugging Face's `ml-intern` is an open-source ML engineer designed to autonomously read research papers, train models, and deploy complete machine learning solutions.

github_trending.description: Vulnerability scanner written in Go which uses the data provided by https://osv.dev
github_trending.summary: OSV-Scanner is a Go-based vulnerability scanner that leverages the comprehensive vulnerability data from OSV.dev to identify security issues in software projects.

github_trending.description: ALL IN ONE Hacking Tool For Hackers
github_trending.summary: This repository, "Z4nzu/hackingtool," functions as a comprehensive, all-in-one hacking toolkit designed to consolidate various utilities for security professionals and hackers.

github_trending.description: Code search MCP for Claude Code. Make entire codebase the context for any coding agent.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a code search mechanism for Claude Code, enabling large language models to utilize an entire codebase as context for coding agent tasks.

github_trending.description: OpenMetadata is a unified metadata platform for data discovery, data observability, and data governance powered by a central metadata repository, in-depth column level lineage, and seamless team collaboration.
github_trending.summary: OpenMetadata is a unified metadata platform providing data discovery, observability, and governance through a central repository, detailed column-level lineage, and collaborative team features.

github_trending.description: 🦔 PostHog is an all-in-one developer platform for building successful products. We offer product analytics, web analytics, session replay, error tracking, feature flags, experimentation, surveys, data warehouse, a CDP, and an AI product assistant to help debug your code, ship features faster, and keep all your usage and customer data in one stack.
github_trending.summary: PostHog is an all-in-one developer platform providing product analytics, session replay, feature flags, experimentation, and more, enabling teams to build successful products by centralizing usage data and accelerating feature delivery.

github_trending.description: Unofficial Bitwarden compatible server written in Rust, formerly known as bitwarden_rs
github_trending.summary: Vaultwarden is an unofficial, Rust-based, lightweight server compatible with the Bitwarden password manager ecosystem, offering a self-hostable alternative.

github_trending.description: Uncensored, open-source alternative to Higgsfield AI, Freepik AI, Krea AI, Openart AI — Free, unrestricted AI image & video generation studio with 200+ models (Flux, Midjourney, Kling, Sora, Veo). No content filters. Self-hosted, MIT licensed.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a free, self-hosted, and uncensored open-source alternative for AI image and video generation, featuring over 200 models like Flux, Midjourney, Kling, Sora, and Veo, all under the permissive MIT license.
